name: Google Ads
access_notes: Developers can access the API by having a Google Ads manager account (MCC) and obtaining a developer token. The 'Explorer' access tier allows for instant access without an application or review process.

evidence.claim: The Google Ads API is a programmatic interface for managing large or complex Google Ads accounts and campaigns.
The 'Explorer' access tier provides instant production access for developers with a manager account (MCC) without an application or review process.
Search citation: Google Ads API - Google Ads Help
Search citation: [Live Demo] Building a Google Ads API Web App - Part 1: What we’re Building

api_notes: The API provides comprehensive programmatic control over accounts, campaigns, keywords, reporting, and billing. It uses Google Ads Query Language (GAQL) for queries. Documentation is extensive.
mcp_notes: Google provides an official MCP server that allows AI tools to execute read-only GAQL queries and list accessible customers.
auth_notes: The API primarily uses OAuth2 for authentication. Applications use OAuth2 flows to obtain tokens tied to the authenticated user's permissions on the Google Ads account.
one_liner: The Google Ads API is a programmatic interface that allows developers to manage, report on, and automate Google Ads accounts and campaigns.
